wpcs_compliance.analysis: PHP magic method __get() implementation. Missing strict comparison in in_array() — should use in_array($name, $this->compat_fields, true). PHPDoc present with @since 4.0.0.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. Pure property accessor.

security.analysis: in_array() without strict=true risks type-coercion bypass in PHP. Property access via $this->$name uses dynamic property access which requires $compat_fields to be tightly controlled.

performance.analysis: Single in_array() on likely small $compat_fields array. O(n) but negligible. Property access is O(1).

wp_api_usage.analysis: Pure PHP magic method. No WordPress API needed. Correct WordPress backward compatibility accessor pattern.

code_quality.analysis: Missing explicit return null for non-matching properties — implicit null return reduces code clarity. Loose in_array() comparison.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Self-contained compatibility accessor. Depends on $compat_fields being initialized by constructor.

i18n.analysis: No translatable strings. Backend property accessor.

accessibility.analysis: No HTML output. Backend accessor.

reasoning.key_observation: Magic __get() backward compatibility accessor with loose in_array() (missing strict=true) and implicit null return on no-match.
